# io-functions-commons — Copilot Instructions

Shared TypeScript library with CosmosDB models, HTTP middlewares, and OpenAPI-generated types for the IO (IO App) Azure Functions platform.

## Build & Test

```sh
yarn build            # clean + generate OpenAPI types + tsc
yarn generate         # run all generate:* scripts (OpenAPI codegen only)
yarn test             # jest unit tests (runs serially with -i)
yarn test:coverage    # jest with coverage report
yarn lint             # eslint
```

Integration tests require local services (CosmosDB emulator, Azurite, Mailhog):

```sh
# See README.md for full docker/env setup
COSMOSDB_URI=https://localhost:3000/ COSMOSDB_KEY="dummy key" ... yarn test:integration
```

> **Do not edit** files under `generated/` — they are auto-generated by `yarn generate` from YAML specs in `openapi/`.

## Architecture

```
openapi/          OpenAPI YAML specs (source of truth for types)
  index.yaml      → generated/definitions/v2/
  index_v3.yaml   → generated/definitions/v3/
  lollipop_definitions.yaml → generated/definitions/lollipop/

generated/        Auto-generated io-ts types from OpenAPI specs (DO NOT EDIT)

src/
  models/         Domain entities backed by Azure CosmosDB (profile, message, service, …)
  utils/          Reusable utilities:
    cosmosdb_model.ts           Base CosmosDB CRUD model
    cosmosdb_model_versioned.ts Version-append model (immutable history)
    cosmosdb_model_ttl.ts       TTL-aware model variant
    middlewares/                Express/Azure Functions request middlewares
    response.ts                 Custom IResponse helpers
    errors.ts, types.ts, …
  mailer/         Nodemailer-based multi-transport email client
  entities/       Lightweight value objects (no DB backing)

__integrations__/ Self-contained jest integration test project
templates/        MJML email templates (compiled to HTML at build time)
```

## Key Patterns

### io-ts + fp-ts (functional style)
All types are defined with **io-ts** (`t.interface`, `t.union`, `t.intersection`, `t.partial`). Runtime validation always returns `Either<Errors, T>`. Business logic is composed with **fp-ts** `pipe`, `TaskEither (TE)`, `Option (O)`, `Either (E)`.

```ts
// Preferred style
import * as TE from "fp-ts/lib/TaskEither";
import { pipe } from "fp-ts/lib/function";

const result = pipe(
  someTaskEither,
  TE.chain(value => doSomething(value)),
  TE.mapLeft(err => toAppError(err))
);
```

Avoid imperative `try/catch` in business logic; use `TE.tryCatch` instead.

### CosmosDB Models
Models extend `CosmosdbModel` (or `CosmosdbModelVersioned` for append-only history). Use `DocumentSearchKey` to type lookup tuples.

```ts
// Defining a model
export class MyModel extends CosmosdbModel<MyType, NewMyType, RetrievedMyType, ...> { ... }

// Versioned model — never updates documents, appends new versions
export class ProfileModel extends CosmosdbModelVersioned<Profile, ...> { ... }
```

- `COLLECTION_NAME` and `MODEL_PK_FIELD` are declared as `const` at module level
- Retrieved documents include `AzureCosmosResource` fields (`_etag`, `_rid`, `_self`, `_ts`)

### Generated Types
Import generated types from `../../generated/definitions/v2/` (or `v3/`, `lollipop/`). Never hand-write types that should come from OpenAPI; update the YAML spec and regenerate instead.

### Middlewares
All request validation is done through composable middlewares in `src/utils/middlewares/`. Handlers return `IResponse<T>` from `@pagopa/ts-commons/lib/responses`.

## Conventions

- **Functional style** throughout — avoid classes except for CosmosDB models which require them
- **`pipe` and `flow`** from fp-ts/function for composition
- **`t.TypeOf<typeof MyType>`** to derive the TS type from an io-ts codec
- **`withDefault`** from `@pagopa/ts-commons/lib/types` for optional fields with defaults
- **Error types** are `CosmosErrors` (union of query/decoding errors); surface as `left` in `TaskEither`
- **Strict TypeScript**: `strict: true`, `noImplicitAny: true` — no `any` without comment justification
- ESLint extends `@pagopa/eslint-config/strong`; generated, test, and mock files are excluded from linting

## Pitfalls

- `generated/` is gitignored-equivalent for edits — always regenerate with `yarn generate`
- Integration tests live in `__integrations__/` as a **separate yarn workspace** with its own `package.json` and `node_modules`; run `yarn install` inside it separately or via `yarn test:integration` which does it automatically
- `peerDependencies` (`fp-ts`, `io-ts`, `express`, `@azure/functions`, `@pagopa/ts-commons`) must not be bundled; consuming projects provide them
- The `build` script runs `tsc` including `generated/**/*` (see `tsconfig.json` include); failing generated types will break the build
- Versioned models (`CosmosdbModelVersioned`) do **not** update existing documents — they append a new document with an incremented `version` field
